summary: Two-story rectangular residential-style building with a stepped brick gable roof, continuous horizontal window bands on both floors, corner stone pillars, and a raised white trim base around the footprint. Exterior walls use a lighter block (sandstone or similar) with a wood plank band between floors and a white horizontal band above and below the windows.

shape.overall_form: simple rectangular block with a symmetric pitched/stepped gable along the long axis
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric along the long centerline (front/back faces mirror)

elements: continuous horizontal window bands (three or four glass panes repeated) on both floors
entrance unclear/not visible in shown views
stepped brick gable roof with central small chimney or cap
white slab or block trim base surrounding footprint
rebuild_hints: Footprint is rectangular; build a two-block-tall sandstone wall band for each floor with a one-block-high wood plank strip between floors.
Place a continuous row of glass windows on each floor across the long face with a repeating 1x1 pane gap pattern (windows appear 4 to 5 panes long per face).
Cap wall with a one-block-high white band (quartz or similar) and create a centrally peaked stepped brick gable roof along the long axis, stepping down one block per step toward eaves.
Add darker stone corner pillars rising full height where the white bands meet corners.
Surround base with a one-block-high white trim (slab or full blocks) raised slightly above surrounding ground.
uncertainties: Exact block types for light-colored walls: appear to be sandstone but could be smooth sandstone or another light block (confidence 0.85).
White trim material likely quartz or white slab but could be diorite or smooth stone (confidence ~0.7).
Exact internal floor heights and presence/location of doors/entrances are not visible in provided images.
Dimension estimates (width/depth/height) are approximate based on visible block counts from images; actual counts may differ by a few blocks.
